title: allium idahoense
intro: Allium idahoense, also known as Idaho onion or Idaho allium, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Idaho and Montana in the United States. It is a member of the Allium genus, which includes various types of onions, garlic, and leeks. The medicinal properties of Allium idahoense include its antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help to boost the immune system and fight off infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is said to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting respiratory health.
